steps.title: Find the growth rate p of f(x)
steps.content: For $f(x) = k(1.84)^x$, increasing $x$ by 1 multiplies the output by 1.84: $$f(x+1) = k(1.84)^{x+1} = 1.84 \cdot k(1.84)^x = 1.84 \cdot f(x).$$ So $f(x)$ increases by $p = 84\%$ for every unit increase in $x$.

steps.title: Determine what g(x) must look like
steps.content: We need $g(x)$ to increase by $84\%$ — that is, multiply by $1.84$ — for every increase of $x$ by 4. So the ratio $g(x+4)/g(x)$ must equal $1.84$. Writing $g(x) = k \cdot (1.84)^{h(x)}$, we need the exponent to increase by exactly $1$ when $x$ increases by $4$, which means $h(x) = x/4$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A: $k(1.84^x)^{1/4} = k \cdot 1.84^{x/4}$. When $x \to x+4$: exponent goes from $x/4$ to $(x+4)/4 = x/4 + 1$, so it multiplies by $1.84^1 = 1.84$. Increases by $84\%$ every 4 steps. ✓
  • Choice B: $k(1.84^x)^4 = k \cdot 1.84^{4x}$. When $x \to x+4$: multiplies by $1.84^{16}$, far more than $84\%$ — eliminate.
  • Choice C: $k(1.84)^{x-4}$. When $x \to x+4$: multiplies by $1.84^{(x+4-4)}/1.84^{(x-4)} = 1.84^1$. Wait — this also multiplies by $1.84$? No: $g(x) = k(1.84)^{x-4}$ means $g(x+4) = k(1.84)^x$ and $g(x) = k(1.84)^{x-4}$, ratio = $1.84^4 \neq 1.84$ — eliminate.
  • Choice D: $k(1.84)^{x+4}$. Ratio $g(x+4)/g(x) = 1.84^{(x+8)}/1.84^{(x+4)} = 1.84^4 \neq 1.84$ — eliminate.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
